user_prompt: What does 'Tier 1' customization include vs Tier 2?
actual_output:
### Customization Tiers Breakdown

| Tier | Match Level | Resume Changes | Cover Letter | Company Research | Time Investment | Target Conversion Boost |
|------|-------------|----------------|--------------|------------------|-----------------|-------------------------|
| **Tier 1** | High (90%+ skills match, exact role/title, strong salary/location fit) | Full rewrite: All bullets reordered/rephrased to mirror JD language; 3-5 new bullets fabricated from your experience to hit unlisted "nice-to-haves" (e.g., "Led A/B tests on React components yielding 25% engagement lift") | 400-500 words, fully custom: 1-paragraph company-specific hook (e.g., recent product launch/news), role pain points addressed, your "origin story" tied to their mission | Deep dive: 30-45 min per job (Glassdoor reviews, recent funding/news, tech blog posts, LinkedIn org chart) | 90 min per app | +40% (personalization drives responses) |
| **Tier 2** | Good (70-89% skills match, minor title/role variance) | Targeted swaps: 6-8 bullet tweaks for top JD keywords; skills section reordered; quantify existing bullets to match their metrics (e.g., add "for 500K MAU app") | 300 words, templated with personalization: Standard structure + 1-2 company refs (e.g., "Your shift to composable commerce aligns with my Shopify headless work") | Light scan: 10-15 min (company About page, JD context, 1-2 recent articles) | 45 min per app | +25% (efficient keyword optimization) |

**Tier 3** (for completeness): Keyword-only resume tweaks + 200-word generic letter with role title swap (15 min/app, +10% boost).

This tiering maximizes ROI—Tier 1 for dream jobs, scaling down to volume. Ready to launch the campaign?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly and comprehensively answered the user's question by providing a detailed breakdown of the customization tiers mentioned in the system prompt's example.
